mission.title: Install and Configure Grafana Operator
mission.description: The Grafana Operator simplifies the management of Grafana instances, dashboards, and data sources in Kubernetes. It is ideal for users who prefer GitOps workflows or infrastructure as code. Key trade-offs include the need for familiarity with Kubernetes CRDs and potential complexity in multi-instance setups.

steps.title: Add the Grafana Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the Grafana Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration, allowing you to install the Grafana Operator.

```bash

helm repo add grafana-operator oci://ghcr.io/grafana/helm-charts

helm repo update

```

> This command sets up the repository for Grafana Operator.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install the Grafana Operator
steps.description:
This command installs the Grafana Operator in your Kubernetes cluster using Helm.

```bash

helm upgrade -i grafana-operator oci://ghcr.io/grafana/helm-charts/grafana-operator --version 5.22.2

```

> Installs the specified version of the Grafana Operator.

steps.title: Create a Grafana instance
steps.description:
This step creates a custom resource (CR) for a Grafana instance, defining its configuration.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: grafana.integreatly.org/v1beta1
kind: Grafana
metadata:
  name: grafana
  labels:
    dashboards: "grafana"
spec:
  config:
    log:
      mode: "console"
    security:
      admin_user: root
      admin_password: <REDACTED>
EOF

```

> Creates a Grafana instance with basic configuration.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: grafana.integreatly.org/v1beta1
kind: Grafana
metadata:
  name: grafana
  labels:
    dashboards: "grafana"
spec:
  config:
    log:
      mode: "console"
    security:
      admin_user: root
      admin_password: <REDACTED>
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Grafana instance
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the Grafana instance to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get grafana grafana -o yaml

```

> Displays the details of the Grafana instance.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the Grafana Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Grafana Operator from your cluster. Be aware that this may lead to data loss if Grafana instances are not backed up.

```bash

helm uninstall grafana-operator

kubectl delete grafana grafana

```

> Uninstalls the Grafana Operator.
> Deletes the Grafana instance, which may result in data loss.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Grafana Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your Grafana instances. This step upgrades the Grafana Operator to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get grafana grafana -o yaml > grafana-backup.yaml

helm upgrade grafana-operator oci://ghcr.io/grafana/helm-charts/grafana-operator --version 5.22.2

kubectl get grafana grafana -o yaml

```

> Backup the current Grafana instance configuration.
> Upgrades the Grafana Operator to the specified version.
> Verify that the Grafana instance is still running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get grafana grafana -o yaml > grafana-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Error: unable to recognize "": no matches for kind "Grafana" in version "grafana.integreatly.org/v1beta1"'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Grafana CRD has not been installed or is not recognized.

**Fix:**
Ensure the Grafana Operator is installed correctly and the CRDs are applied.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to Grafana: 404 Not Found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This suggests that the Grafana instance is not running or has failed to start.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the Grafana pod using 'kubectl logs <grafana-pod-name>'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Grafana instance is not ready'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Grafana instance may be in the process of starting or has encountered an error.

**Fix:**
Run 'kubectl describe grafana grafana' to check the events and status.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Permission denied'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the service account used by the Grafana Operator does not have sufficient permissions.

**Fix:**
Ensure the service account has the necessary RBAC permissions.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new Grafana features and improved CRD handling.
versionNotes.deprecations: No specific deprecations in this version.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No migration steps are required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the Grafana instance running with the command 'kubectl get grafana' returning the status as 'Running'.

    security:

EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the required resources.
